portfolio_health_check.first_reaction: This is interesting if it really starts from my total portfolio and not from what your bank wants to sell me next.
portfolio_health_check.strongest_appeal: A single view of whole-portfolio risk across currencies, geography, and overlapping holdings, especially if it shows her real China and USD concentration clearly.
portfolio_health_check.biggest_concern: That linking external assets will simply give the bank a more sophisticated way to target product switches and RM outreach.
portfolio_health_check.what_they_understand: She understands the value of whole-portfolio risk, concentration flags, stress testing, scenario analysis, and mismatch checks against a stored risk profile. She also understands why multi-currency exposures can distort how safe a portfolio looks at product level.
portfolio_health_check.what_they_misunderstand: She may over-interpret 'institutional-grade' as meaning the tool is more predictive than it really is, and she may not initially distinguish between scenario analysis, probability, and recommendation.
portfolio_health_check.data_they_would_share: Read-only holdings, approximate cost basis if needed for better analytics, currency balances, MPF allocation at a broad level, and external brokerage positions if permissions are revocable and clearly explained.
portfolio_health_check.data_they_would_not_share: Full transaction history unless clearly necessary, spouse or joint-account details by default, beneficiary information, unrelated insurance-policy personal details, and any access that allows execution rather than analysis.
portfolio_health_check.preferred_explanation_format: Layered bilingual output: one plain-language summary page, then expandable charts for currency exposure, geography, and concentration, followed by scenario explanations in short bullets and one downloadable summary she can revisit later.
portfolio_health_check.whether_rm_needed: Helpful but not needed for the first pass. She wants to review the analysis alone first, then use an RM only if a finding is material or ambiguous.
portfolio_health_check.whether_they_would_pay: Possibly, but not upfront. She would prefer it included in an existing banking tier and would only pay separately if the feature repeatedly reveals useful blind spots and remains clearly distinct from sales activity.
portfolio_health_check.what_would_make_them_try: A sample report on a realistic mock portfolio, read-only account aggregation, a visible toggle for RM visibility, and proof that external holdings receive the same quality of analysis as bank-held assets.
portfolio_health_check.what_would_make_them_abandon: Product recommendations appearing too early, repeated sales follow-up after linking data, stale or incomplete external asset coverage, or risk explanations that sound grand but cannot answer practical follow-up questions.
